content:
I have a wide array of skills all hovering around my understanding of how to weave a story.













Here’s the problem: no one can talk about themselves accurately. Resumes and “About Me”s only paint half a picture of what you can only hope to be a full person. So while acknowledging this impossibility, I suppose it only makes sense to absurdly proceed forward foolheartedly. Since The Fool is the zero-th card of the Tarot, let’s start from the beginning.

I’m not entirely sure how I was conceived, but I do know my mom’s water broke in a Blockbuster. Which, trust me, explains a lot. That and my birthmark above my left eye (see: Mark of Cain).

So of course being born in 1990, I was raised on a steady diet of new media. Television for three square meals a day, and video games when they stopped looking like Pong and started looking like Goldeneye.

Tucked into my Tommy Hilfiger bedsheets at the sprite-ful age of—oh, I don’t know—since-I-can-ever-remember, I was subject to the maternal tradition of being read to until I fell asleep. I think my mom wanted to insure I didn’t stay up all night playing Gameboy. Or she just wanted to spend time with her first born son.
context: From document: Lets cut to the chase. You having to read this is as  much a waste… — chunk 2 (chunk 2/3)
